senses.gloss: verbal noun of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to be reasonable”) (form I)
understanding, reason, wisdom, sound judgment, sense

examples.text: حَدِّثِ الْعَاقِلَ بِمَا لَا يَلِيقُ، فَإِنْ صَدَّقَ فَلَا عَقْلَ لَهُ.
examples.english: Tell the intelligent man what does not befit him and if he deems it credible he has no sense.

senses.gloss: to be reasonable, to make sense
to make oneself inaccessible in a high mountain, to betake oneself for refuge
verbal noun of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to find refuge in a high mountain”) (form I)
verbal noun of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to renounce revenge for wergild”) (form I)
to renounce revenge for wergild, to relinquish retaliation of blood
to hobble, to tie with the rope عِقَال (ʕiqāl)
verbal noun of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to hobble”) (form I)
to have a twisting in the hind leg
verbal noun of عَقِلَ (ʕaqila, “to have a twisting in the hind leg”) (form I)
verbal noun of عَقَلَ (ʕaqala, “to pasture upon camelthorn”) (form I)
